mappings.rationale: Directly covers population composition, fertility, mortality, migration, and population theories.
Directly covers urban planning methods, materials, and social aspects of urban redevelopment (subsection B.3).
Directly covers suburbanization and the growth of residential communities surrounding cities (subsection B.4.b).

mappings.vsiAuthor: John A. Matthews, David T. Herbert
mappings.rationale: Covers spatial distribution of populations and the relationship between urban and rural communities.
Directly covers migration as a determinant of population composition and change (subsection A.1.c).
Covers refugee movements as a form of migration affecting population composition (subsection A.1.c).
Covers environmental change including pollution and climatic change as problems of urban growth (subsection B.4.f).
Covers the economic relationship between population growth, resource conservation, and environmental problems (subsections A.3.g and B.4.f).
Covers ethical dimensions of population growth and environmental change in urban contexts.
Covers political responses to problems of urban growth, pollution, and population control (subsection B.4.e).
Covers sustainable development in relation to population growth and urban environmental challenges.
Directly covers Malthusian population theory (subsection A.3.d).
Covers mortality rates and public health dimensions of population studies (subsection A.1.b).
Covers public health infrastructure affecting mortality and longevity in urban populations.
Covers pandemics as events affecting mortality rates and population change.
Covers how global economic forces drive urbanization, migration, and megalopolis formation.
Covers the built environment of cities, relevant to methods and materials of urban planning (subsection B.3.a).
Covers modern architectural approaches to urban design and city planning.
Covers landscape design within urban planning and the relationship between cities and their environments.
Covers ecological theories relevant to the relationship between population growth and natural resource conservation (subsection A.3.g).
Covers the impact of urbanization and population growth on the environment, relevant to problems of environmental change.
Covers the social dimensions of urban segregation, relevant to social aspects of urban planning (subsection B.3.b).
Covers regional integration and the relationship between population, territory, and political organization.
Covers population movements and the formation of diaspora communities in urban centres.
Covers the historical forces driving urbanization and the growth of industrial cities (subsection B.2).
Covers sociological perspectives on urbanization, community structure, and social effects of population change.
